text:
There was once a barren land filled with nothing but jagged rocks and thorny bushes. Life was impossible on this rocky shore—sunlight could not reach the ground, plants could not find nutrients, and creatures could not find safe nesting grounds. Amidst the desolation, a wise old oak tree stood firm, its roots entangled with the roots of a dead tree from which it had emerged. This oak knew the secrets to survival and decided to share its wisdom.

Over seasons, the wise oak taught the other plants how to capture the scarce rainwater, how to convert sunlight into energy through photosynthesis, and introduced seedlings to the most fertile spots in the barren expanse. Even the insects that buzzed among the rocks were taught to avoid the worst parts and sought out the few flowers offering nectar.

As time passed, small green sprouts replaced the dead plant, and lichens started clinging to the rocks. Life transformed from the impossible—a fragile but growing green canopy—and the rocky
